description: You are a Compensation Analyst for a retail chain auditing 'Fair Workweek' compliance using the HR_COMPLIANCE schema in Oracle (hr_compliance/Compliance2024, XEPDB1, localhost:1521). Detect violations and calculate statutory penalties from the scheduling and time-punch data. A view named CLOPENING_VIOLATIONS_VW must identify instances where an employee had less than 10.0 hours of rest between consecutive shifts (excluding shifts with missing clock-out times), containing emp_id, shift_id, previous_shift_id, rest_hours (rounded to 2 decimals), and a fixed penalty_amount of $50. A view named PREDICTIVE_SCHEDULING_VW must identify worked shifts where the schedule was modified less than 14.0 days before the planned start, containing emp_id, schedule_id, notice_days (rounded to 2 decimals), and a penalty_amount of one hour of the employee's hourly_rate. A view named MEAL_BREAK_VIOLATIONS_VW must identify shifts exceeding 7.0 hours where the unpaid meal break was less than 30 minutes or missing, containing emp_id, shift_id, shift_hours (rounded to 2 decimals), break_minutes (rounded to 0 decimals, using 0 for missing values), and a penalty_amount of one hour of hourly_rate. A materialized view named PAYROLL_PENALTY_SUMMARY_MV must aggregate all three penalty types per employee, showing emp_id, total_clopening_penalty, total_scheduling_penalty, total_meal_penalty, and grand_total_penalty (using 0 for categories with no penalties). Export the final summary to /home/ga/Documents/exports/fair_workweek_penalties.csv with headers.

metadata.occupation: Compensation, Benefits, and Job Analysis Specialists
